verses.text: Hold the faith 'without respect of persons'; do not honour the rich and slight the poor, becoming 'judges of evil thoughts.' No favouritism. Partiality denies the gospel of grace.
Hold the faith 'without respect of persons'; do not honour the rich and slight the poor, becoming 'judges of evil thoughts.' No favouritism. Partiality denies the gospel of grace.
Hold the faith 'without respect of persons'; do not honour the rich and slight the poor, becoming 'judges of evil thoughts.' No favouritism. Partiality denies the gospel of grace.
Hold the faith 'without respect of persons'; do not honour the rich and slight the poor, becoming 'judges of evil thoughts.' No favouritism. Partiality denies the gospel of grace.
God 'chosen the poor of this world rich in faith'; yet you despise the poor, while the rich oppress you. God's choice. He exalts the lowly believer.
God 'chosen the poor of this world rich in faith'; yet you despise the poor, while the rich oppress you. God's choice. He exalts the lowly believer.
God 'chosen the poor of this world rich in faith'; yet you despise the poor, while the rich oppress you. God's choice. He exalts the lowly believer.
Fulfil 'the royal law... Thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself'; to respect persons is to commit sin. Love's rule. Partiality breaks the law of love.
Fulfil 'the royal law... Thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself'; to respect persons is to commit sin. Love's rule. Partiality breaks the law of love.
Whoever keeps the whole law yet offends in one point 'is guilty of all.' One breach. The law is one, and any sin makes a man a transgressor.
Whoever keeps the whole law yet offends in one point 'is guilty of all.' One breach. The law is one, and any sin makes a man a transgressor.
So speak and do as those to be judged by 'the law of liberty'; 'he shall have judgment without mercy, that hath shewed no mercy.' Mercy wins. The merciful find mercy at the judgment.
So speak and do as those to be judged by 'the law of liberty'; 'he shall have judgment without mercy, that hath shewed no mercy.' Mercy wins. The merciful find mercy at the judgment.
What profit if a man says he has faith but has no works? 'faith, if it hath not works, is dead.' Living faith. Real faith proves itself in deeds.
What profit if a man says he has faith but has no works? 'faith, if it hath not works, is dead.' Living faith. Real faith proves itself in deeds.
What profit if a man says he has faith but has no works? 'faith, if it hath not works, is dead.' Living faith. Real faith proves itself in deeds.
What profit if a man says he has faith but has no works? 'faith, if it hath not works, is dead.' Living faith. Real faith proves itself in deeds.
Show me thy faith 'by thy works'; the devils believe and tremble. 'faith without works is dead.' Mere assent. Bare belief is no better than a demon's.
Show me thy faith 'by thy works'; the devils believe and tremble. 'faith without works is dead.' Mere assent. Bare belief is no better than a demon's.
Show me thy faith 'by thy works'; the devils believe and tremble. 'faith without works is dead.' Mere assent. Bare belief is no better than a demon's.
Abraham was 'justified by works' when he offered Isaac, and faith was 'made perfect.' By works a man is justified. Proven faith. True faith and obedience go together.
Abraham was 'justified by works' when he offered Isaac, and faith was 'made perfect.' By works a man is justified. Proven faith. True faith and obedience go together.
Abraham was 'justified by works' when he offered Isaac, and faith was 'made perfect.' By works a man is justified. Proven faith. True faith and obedience go together.
Abraham was 'justified by works' when he offered Isaac, and faith was 'made perfect.' By works a man is justified. Proven faith. True faith and obedience go together.
Rahab too was justified by works; 'as the body without the spirit is dead, so faith without works is dead also.' Dead faith. Faith without deeds is a lifeless corpse.
